mnemonics.title: C-SPACES Model
mnemonics.content: C-SPACES stands for: Compute, Storage, PaaS Services, Analytics, Cloud Networking, Embedded Security — the main Azure service categories

mnemonics.title: IPS Triangle
mnemonics.content: IPS = Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), Software as a Service (SaaS) — the three cloud deployment models, from most to least control

mnemonics.title: BLOB-FILE-QUEUE-TABLE
mnemonics.content: B-F-Q-T = Azure Storage types: Blob (unstructured), File (file shares), Queue (messaging), Table (NoSQL) — remember as 'Big Files Queue To Table'

mnemonics.title: Azure VM Lifecycle
mnemonics.content: CRUD-VM = Create, Run, Use, Deallocate, Delete — the lifecycle stages of an Azure Virtual Machine

mnemonics.title: ARM Template Reminder
mnemonics.content: ARM = Azure Resource Manager — think 'A' for 'All' resources managed in one place, like a central command center

mnemonics.title: Azure AD Powers
mnemonics.content: IAM-RBAC-MFA = Identity and Access Management, Role-Based Access Control, Multi-Factor Authentication — the security trio

acronyms.usage: Use when renting virtual machines, storage, and networking — you manage OS and apps, Azure manages hardware
Use when building apps without managing infrastructure — Azure provides runtime, database, and development tools
Use when using ready-to-use applications like Microsoft 365 — no installation, subscription-based
Use to create isolated network environments in Azure for VMs and services to communicate securely
Use for deploying and managing resources in Azure through templates (Infrastructure as Code)
Use to assign permissions to users based on their roles (Owner, Contributor, Reader)
Use for identity management and single sign-on across cloud applications
Use to add extra security layer — requires two or more verification methods
Use to cache content at edge locations globally for faster delivery to users
Use for running applications in the cloud — like a computer you rent remotely
Use for globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database service
Use to check uptime guarantees — 99.9% means max 8.76 hours downtime/year

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pizza — IaaS: you rent oven and ingredients (you bake); PaaS: you rent oven with dough (Azure bakes); SaaS: you buy ready pizza (eat directly)
Picture a globe with clusters of data centers — each region is a city, availability zones are buildings in that city, connected but physically separated for fault tolerance
Think of aResource Group as a moving box — all related resources (VMs, storage, databases) go in one box for easy management and deletion together
Imagine a warehouse: Blob storage = bulk containers for files; File storage = organized file cabinets; Queue = conveyor belt for messages; Table = spreadsheet for structured data
Draw VNET as an island — the island has its own IP address range, and bridges (VPN Gateway) connect it to the mainland (on-premises network)
Think of Azure AD as a digital security guard — checks identity, issues badges (tokens), and controls who enters which rooms (applications)
Imagine a hotel — you only pay for the room when you use it, not for keeping it empty; Functions run only when triggered
Visualize shipping containers — lightweight, portable, all dependencies packaged together, runs anywhere

analogies.analogy: Like renting a fully furnished apartment — you get a place to live (OS), but the building (hardware) is managed by the landlord (Azure)
Like a massive warehouse for storing random items — videos, images, logs, backups — anything goes, charged per GB stored
Like hiring a gig worker for a single task — they appear when needed, do one job, then disappear; you only pay for the work done
Like a traffic signal at a busy intersection — distributes cars (users) evenly across multiple lanes (servers) to prevent congestion
Like having multiple embassy offices worldwide — whichever one you visit, you get the same documents (data), fast and consistent
Like a secure tunnel under a river — creates an encrypted private path from your office to Azure cloud, keeping data safe
Like a recipe card — write ingredients (resources) once, and anyone can bake (deploy) the exact same infrastructure cake every time
Like a bank account for Azure — all resources are billed to one subscription, and you can set spending limits like a credit card cap
